name: .Astronomy 9
venue: South African Astronomical Observatory, Cape Town
description: The ninth .Astronomy conference was held at the South African Astronomical Observatory (SAAO) in Cape Town, bringing the conference to Africa for the first time. The rapid growth of astronomy in South Africa, with major projects like the SKA, SALT, and the Office of Astronomy for Development, made it an exciting backdrop. The conference focused on how new technologies (machine learning, web interfaces, software development) could help astronomical research, education, and outreach, and how diversity could help us better understand the Universe.

talks.speaker: Bradley Frank
talks.title: Jupyter Notebooks for Radio Astronomy on the Cloud
talks.notes: MeerKAT and SKA data challenges; using cloud computing and Jupyter notebooks for radio interferometric data analysis and training.

talks.speaker: Amanda Bauer
talks.title: LSST: Education and Public Outreach
talks.notes: How LSST EPO will enable public access to survey data via a Portal, Skyviewer, Jupyter notebooks, and citizen science projects.

talks.speaker: Bruce Bassett
talks.title: Threads of a Future Research 3.0
talks.notes: Lessons from experiments in research, collaboration and mentoring: how can we do research better?

talks.speaker: Arna Karick
talks.title: Getting the most out of hack day: Lessons learned from hacking for humanity
talks.notes: Experience as a Random Hacks of Kindness organiser; tips for the .Astronomy 9 Hack Day.

talks.speaker: Wanda Diaz Merced
talks.affiliation: IAU Office of Astronomy for Development
talks.title: Is the use of multimodal perception a real possibility for Astronomy research, education and outreach?
talks.notes: The history and future of sonification and multimodal approaches in astronomy.

talks.speaker: Brian Nord
talks.title: The role of narrative in the culture and practice of science
talks.notes: Storytelling as a fundamental mode of scientific communication and research.

talks.speaker: Temba Matomela
talks.title: The AmaXhosa understanding of the night sky
talks.notes: Indigenous astronomy: Xhosa knowledge of the Pleiades, Orion's Belt, Canopus, and the Moon.

talks.speaker: Susan Murabana
talks.title: The Travelling Telescope
talks.notes: Promoting science and technology in African schools; reached 40,000+ students in Kenya.

talks.speaker: Lisa Ballard
talks.title: Postcards from the Outer Planets
talks.notes: NASA Planetary Data System search interface and API for Voyager, Cassini, New Horizons, and Galileo imagery.

talks.speaker: Fernando Becerra
talks.title: Astrollytelling: Astronomy through visual stories
talks.notes: Using scrollytelling and D3.js to explain astronomy concepts interactively at astrollytelling.io.

talks.speaker: Ben Cook
talks.title: Galaxy photometry modelling, HPC, machine learning, science outreach

talks.speaker: Nicholas Earl
talks.title: Science software for JWST; circumgalactic medium research

talks.speaker: Kevin Govender
talks.affiliation: IAU Office of Astronomy for Development
talks.title: Astronomy for development in Africa

talks.speaker: Brett Morris
talks.title: Astropy affiliated package astroplan; stellar activity and exoplanets

talks.speaker: Brian Nord
talks.title: AI applications in cosmology; science communication workshops

talks.speaker: Josh Peek
talks.title: Project Scientist, Data Science Mission Office; ISM and MAST

talks.speaker: Brigitta Sipocz
talks.title: Astropy Project; exoplanets, low mass stars, asteroids

talks.speaker: Becky Smethurst
talks.title: Galaxy quenching and AGN feedback; YouTube science communicator

hacks.title: Hack Graveyard
hacks.description: .Astronomy is full of amazing pitches and hacks. Unfortunately, there are never enough hours in the Hack Day to complete all of them. The Hack Graveyard tracks these incomplete hacks so they can be revived in the future.

hacks.title: Hack Roulette
hacks.description: Spin the wheel to generate a new hack using past dotastro hacks.

hacks.title: Project PHaEDRA Website and Logo
hacks.description: Rethinking and rough drafting a logo and website for Project PHaEDRA: a collaborative effort to digitize and transcribe the work of early astronomers.

hacks.title: Sketch dotAstro: Hunting for Jupiter Sketches in Archival Notebooks
hacks.description: Attempting to use computer vision to distinguish between boring pages in archival notebooks and pages with sketches of Jupiter.

hacks.title: Wave At Kepler
hacks.description: Static website to compute the direction to Kepler when it takes a photo, so you can wave at the right moment.

hacks.title: Where in the Universe?
hacks.description: Have you ever wondered where you'd be in the Universe if you were launched toward the zenith above your birth place at the time you were born? This site calculates your position in space and the nearest star.

hacks.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
hacks.description: A small package for plotting common spectral line lists on your spectra, so you can discover which absorption/emission features are responsible for what you see.

hacks.title: xorastro: Extremely Open, Extremely Reproducible Astronomy
hacks.description: Built a neural network to predict galaxy colors from galaxy shapes in the most open and reproducible way possible.

links.label: bmorris3/dotastro9_astroplan_astroquery
links.description: tutorial materials for astroplan and astroquery

programme_notes: Themes included machine learning, web interfaces, software development, education, and leveraging diversity in the astronomical community. This was the first .Astronomy held in Africa.
